skill.name: context-manager
skill.description: Guides context engineering for AI systems, including context assembly, retrieval, memory design, knowledge graphs, and multi-agent context handoffs.
skill.summary: Design and improve context management for AI agents, RAG systems, and long-running workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings to be false positives in prose-only Markdown. SKILL.md line 27 references an optional resource file in inline code, line 53 describes hybrid search, and the reported weak cryptography lines contain ordinary context-management text rather than cryptographic algorithms. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable code, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: AI teams often lose quality when context becomes stale, too large, or poorly routed. This skill provides structured guidance for context architecture, retrieval, memory, and multi-agent handoffs.
actual_capabilities: Plans dynamic context assembly for AI applications and agent workflows.
Recommends retrieval patterns for vector search, keyword search, and hybrid search.
Designs memory structures for conversations, projects, and persistent knowledge.
Maps context handoff patterns for multi-agent and multi-stage workflows.
Defines quality, freshness, cost, and performance checks for context systems.
Outlines governance considerations for enterprise context storage and access.
limitations: Provides guidance and architecture patterns, not a working retrieval engine.
Does not include implementation files beyond the Markdown instruction set.
Requires project-specific data, privacy rules, and infrastructure choices from the user.
May need current vendor documentation for exact database or model configuration.

use_cases.title: Plan Enterprise RAG Architecture
use_cases.description: Design chunking, retrieval, freshness, and evaluation practices for a large document search system.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Define context routing, handoff rules, and shared memory boundaries across specialized agents.

use_cases.title: Improve Long-Running Assistant Memory
use_cases.description: Create a memory strategy that separates active context, user preferences, facts, and archived history.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Context Problem
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis of a context quality issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AI workflow and identify where context is missing, stale, duplicated, or too large. Ask for any required details first.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Retrieval Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a retrieval plan for my document corpus. Include chunking, metadata, hybrid search, ranking, freshness, and evaluation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Memory Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a memory architecture for a long-running AI assistant. Separate working, episodic, semantic, and archival memory with update rules.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Multi-Agent Context Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this multi-agent workflow for context handoff risk, token waste, privacy exposure, conflicting state, and recovery gaps.

output_examples.input: A support assistant gives inconsistent answers across long customer cases.
output: A context map that separates customer profile, current case, policy knowledge, and prior interactions.
A retrieval plan with freshness rules, source ranking, and fallback behavior.
A validation checklist for answer consistency and privacy boundaries.

output_examples.input: A team needs several agents to analyze, draft, and review technical documentation.
output: A handoff model that defines what each agent receives and returns.
A shared memory boundary for project facts, decisions, and open questions.
Quality checks for missing context, duplicate summaries, and stale assumptions.

output_examples.input: A RAG system retrieves too many irrelevant passages from enterprise documents.
output: A diagnosis of chunking, metadata, query expansion, and ranking weaknesses.
A revised retrieval strategy using filters, hybrid search, and relevance scoring.
An evaluation plan with representative queries and acceptance criteria.

best_practices: Define context ownership, freshness, and retention rules before selecting storage tools.
Measure retrieval quality with representative tasks, not only similarity scores.
Keep sensitive data boundaries explicit in memory, retrieval, and agent handoff designs.
anti_patterns: Do not store all conversation history as active context without pruning or ranking.
Do not mix user preferences, facts, and temporary task state in one memory layer.
Do not optimize token usage without checking answer quality and source coverage.

faq.question: Does this skill build a vector database for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance, design checks, and implementation planning for vector database use.

faq.question: Can it help with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It is written for AI context planning and supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code usage patterns.

faq.question: Is this skill only for RAG systems?
faq.answer: No. It also covers memory systems, knowledge graphs, multi-agent coordination, and context window optimization.

faq.question: What inputs make the skill more useful?
faq.answer: Provide the workflow goal, user types, data sources, privacy constraints, latency needs, and failure examples.

faq.question: Does it make security decisions automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can structure privacy and governance considerations, but final security approval needs project review.

faq.question: Can it help reduce token usage?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests pruning, summarization, ranking, and context routing methods to control token use.
